description_long: This guard stone is a roughly hewn, pale stone block with a primitive, stylized animal form—resembling a horse or pony—emerging from one side. The stone has a coarse, uneven texture with visible chisel marks and natural pitting, suggesting it was hand-carved from a single block. The animal figure is squat and robust, with four thick legs and a rounded head, but lacks fine detail, giving it a rustic, ancient appearance. The rear and sides of the stone are irregular and unshaped, contrasting with the more defined front. The object is mounted on a flat, rectangular stone or wooden base, which provides stability and a clear separation from the ground. Its coloration is predominantly off-white to light beige, with subtle variations due to the stone's natural grain and weathering.
description: A roughly carved, pale stone guard stone featuring a primitive animal figure on a rectangular base, with a coarse, weathered surface.
description_view_0: The left side shows the blocky, irregular rear of the stone with rough texture and minimal detail.
description_view_1: The narrow side view highlights the stone's thickness and the deep crevice separating the animal form from the main block.
description_view_2: The front view reveals the stylized animal figure, with four legs and a rounded head, standing out from the block.
description_view_3: The right side displays the animal's head and foreleg, with the rest of the stone receding behind it.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the entrance to driveways, alleyways, or along the edge of urban streets to protect corners and prevent vehicle encroachment onto sidewalks or pedestrian areas.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be fully above ground, standing upright on its base, sometimes partially embedded for stability.
attribute_wear_patterns: Likely to show chipping and smoothing on edges and protruding surfaces due to vehicle contact and foot traffic, with some discoloration or erosion from weather exposure, especially on the upper and outermost areas.
